source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/09_HnVUE/92_인증 - FDA/참고샘플 - Aspenview/02.[AI-AspenView-SAD] Software architecture design (rev.0).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: AspenView software architecture design document describing software modules, DICOM use, and security modules.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports DHF/regulatory evidence for AspenView software architecture, module design, DICOM handling, encryption, and session security behavior.
keyPoints: Doc. No. is AI-AspenView-SAD with initiate date 2022.06.06 and Revision 0.
The software architecture consists of independent modules, with MVVM as the center of the architecture.
The fo-dicom library is used for DICOM.
Security modules include AES key and IV generation, DICOM tag encryption/decryption, password hashing, and unused session termination after 10 minutes.

entities.name: fo-dicom
entities.evidence: Also fo-dicom library is used for DICOM.

entities.name: WPF
entities.evidence: The View, crafted using WPF, is focused on presenting a rich and interactive user interface

entities.name: .NET System.Security.Cryptography
entities.evidence: The key and IV are generated using .NET's System.Security.Cryptography.Aes and System.Security.Cryptography.RandomNumberGenerator.

concepts.name: Software Architecture Design Configuration
concepts.evidence: The software architecture consists some modules and each module functions independently.

concepts.name: MVVM
concepts.evidence: MVVM is the center of the software architecture. MVVM streamlines the software’s architecture, enhancing both modularity and maintainability.

concepts.name: DICOM Module
concepts.evidence: Fo-dicom is an open-source project representing a combination of DICOM (Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine) libraries for .NET, .NET Core, Universal Windows Platform (UWP), and Xamarin.

concepts.name: CreateAesService Module
concepts.evidence: The AES key and IV values are generated when AspenView is first executed and are stored in the WorkList.db.

concepts.name: EncryptDicomParameter
concepts.evidence: This module encrypts DICOM tags. It encrypts the following tags using AES256: InstitutionName, StudyDescription, ReferringPhysiciansName, RequestingPhysician, ReasonForTheRequestedProcedure, ScheduledProcedureStepDescription, InstitutionalDepartmentName, PatientsName, PatientID, PatientsBirthDate, PatientsSex, PatientsAge, PatientsSize, PatientsWeight, OperatorsName, and ResponsiblePerson.

concepts.name: Decrypt DCM File
concepts.evidence: This module decrypts the tags contained in a DCM file. It first opens the DCM file using the fo-dicom module, decrypts each tag, and then saves the result.

concepts.name: CommonUtils.GetCryptedString
concepts.evidence: This function is used for password encryption. It returns a hash value of the input text, utilizing .NET's System.Security.Cryptography.

concepts.name: Unused session termination
concepts.evidence: The user will be automatically logged out if no mouse or keyboard events occur for a certain period of time (10 minutes).

evidence.claim: The document is titled Software Architecture Design for AspenView.
evidence.evidenceText: Doc. Name: AspenView/Doc. No.: AI-AspenView-SAD Initiate date: 2022.06.06 /Revision: 0 Software Architecture Design

evidence.claim: The document initiate date is 2022.06.06 and revision is 0.
Revision status records revision number 0 as firstly prepared on 2022.06.06.
AspenView software architecture consists of independent modules.
evidence.evidenceText: The software architecture consists some modules and each module functions independently.

evidence.claim: MVVM is the center of the AspenView software architecture and is intended to enhance modularity and maintainability.
evidence.evidenceText: MVVM is the center of the software architecture. MVVM streamlines the software’s architecture, enhancing both modularity and maintainability.

evidence.claim: The AspenView Model component manages business logic and data operations.
evidence.evidenceText: This component manages the business logic and data operations specific to AspenView.

evidence.claim: The AspenView View component is crafted using WPF.
evidence.evidenceText: The View, crafted using WPF, is focused on presenting a rich and interactive user interface

evidence.claim: The fo-dicom library is used for DICOM in AspenView.
evidence.evidenceText: Also fo-dicom library is used for DICOM.

evidence.claim: The image processing module provides high quality x-ray images at higher speeds with preset parameters optimized for each shooting area.
evidence.evidenceText: Provides high quality x-ray images at even higher speeds. Various preset parameters are optimized for each shooting area.

evidence.claim: AspenView includes Detector and Generator communication libraries.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector Library to communicate with Detector. Generator Library to communicate with Generator.

evidence.claim: CreateAesService generates AES key and IV values on first execution and stores them in WorkList.db.
evidence.evidenceText: The AES key and IV values are generated when AspenView is first executed and are stored in the WorkList.db.

evidence.claim: EncryptDicomParameter encrypts specified DICOM tags using AES256.
evidence.evidenceText: This module encrypts DICOM tags. It encrypts the following tags using AES256: InstitutionName, StudyDescription, ReferringPhysiciansName, RequestingPhysician, ReasonForTheRequestedProcedure, ScheduledProcedureStepDescription, InstitutionalDepartmentName, PatientsName, PatientID, PatientsBirthDate, PatientsSex, PatientsAge, PatientsSize, PatientsWeight, OperatorsName, and ResponsiblePerson.

evidence.claim: Decrypt DCM File opens a DCM file using fo-dicom, decrypts each tag, and saves the result.
evidence.evidenceText: It first opens the DCM file using the fo-dicom module, decrypts each tag, and then saves the result.

evidence.claim: CommonUtils.GetCryptedString is used for password encryption and returns a hash value of input text.
evidence.evidenceText: This function is used for password encryption. It returns a hash value of the input text, utilizing .NET's System.Security.Cryptography.

evidence.claim: EncryptData and DecryptData create an Encryptor and Decryptor using AES key and IV values.
evidence.evidenceText: Using the AES key and IV values, an Encryptor and Decryptor are created.

evidence.claim: The NotiBar module includes screen saver setting and unused session termination after 10 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: Screen saver function setting and unused session termination. The user will be automatically logged out if no mouse or keyboard events occur for a certain period of time (10 minutes).

evidence.claim: The source metadata identifies FDA as the authority signal.
